title: DAG Plan
description: A language-neutral typed execution graph. Nodes reference entries in a typed registry; edges carry schema-validated data. Each node emits an (artifact, certificate) pair.

task_description.description: The natural language task this DAG was composed for

registry_ref.description: Path or URI to the registry used during composition

registry_hash.description: SHA-256 of the registry file at composition time

provider.description: LLM provider used for composition (e.g. openrouter, groq, session)

id.description: Unique node identifier within this DAG

registry_ref.description: Name of the node in the typed registry

params.description: Static parameters for this node invocation. Must conform to the registry node's param_schema if defined.

input_mappings.description: Maps upstream node outputs to this node's inputs

certificate.description: Certificate specification for this node. When present, the node must emit an (artifact, certificate) pair.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this node does in this DAG

from_port.description: Output field name on the source node

to_port.description: Input field name on the target node

schema_ref.description: JSON Schema describing the data flowing through this edge. Derived from source output schema at validation time.

source_node.description: Node ID to pull data from

source_field.description: Output field on the source node

transform.description: Simple transform applied to the data before passing

predicate_name.description: Name of the deterministic predicate function

predicate_expression.description: Python/JS expression evaluating to bool. Receives `artifact` and `evidence` as variables.

evidence_keys.description: Keys that must be present in the evidence dict for this predicate

required_upstream_certs.description: Node IDs whose certificates must pass before this node executes

on_failure.description: Behavior when the certificate predicate returns false

max_retries.description: Maximum retry count when on_failure is 'retry'
